web_text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy our brother, to the saints and faithful brothers in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ. We give thanks to God the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you, having heard of your faith in Christ Jesus, and of the love which you have toward all the saints,
slang_text: It's Paul here, an apostle of Christ Jesus 'cause that's God's own will, and Timothy our bro, writin' to the saints and faithful brothers in Christ out in Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ. We give thanks to God the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, prayin' for you guys nonstop, ever since we heard about your faith in Christ Jesus and your love for all the saints,

web_text: because of the hope which is laid up for you in the heavens, of which you heard before in the word of the truth of the Good News, which has come to you; even as it is in all the world and is bearing fruit and growing, as it does in you also, since the day you heard and knew the grace of God in truth; even as you learned of Epaphras our beloved fellow servant, who is a faithful servant of Christ on our behalf, who also declared to us your love in the Spirit.
slang_text: all 'cause of the hope stashed away for you up in the heavens, the hope you already heard about in the true word of the Good News that reached you — same as it's spreadin' through the whole world, bearin' fruit and growin', same as it's doin' in you too, ever since the day you heard it and got the grace of God for real; same as you learned it from Epaphras, our beloved fellow servant, a faithful servant of Christ workin' on our behalf, who's the one who told us about your love in the Spirit.

web_text: For this cause, we also, since the day we heard this, don’t cease praying and making requests for you, that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding, that you may walk worthily of the Lord, to please him in all respects, bearing fruit in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God; strengthened with all power, according to the might of his glory, for all endurance and perseverance with joy; giving thanks to the Father, who made us fit to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light;
slang_text: So ever since we heard about it, we haven't quit prayin' and makin' requests for you, that you'd get filled up with knowin' His will, loaded with spiritual wisdom and understandin', so you could walk worthy of the Lord, pleasin' Him every which way, bearin' fruit in every good work, growin' in the knowledge of God; pumped up with all kinds of power, matchin' the might of His glory, stayin' tough and patient with joy; givin' thanks to the Father, who made us fit to share in the inheritance of the saints, up in the light;

web_text: who delivered us out of the power of darkness, and translated us into the Kingdom of the Son of his love; in whom we have our redemption, the forgiveness of our sins; who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation. For by him all things were created, in the heavens and on the earth, things visible and things invisible, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers; all things have been created through him, and for him.
slang_text: who busted us out from darkness' grip and dropped us right into the Kingdom of His beloved Son; in whom we've got our redemption, forgiveness for all our sins; the exact image of the invisible God, the firstborn over every bit of creation. 'Cause everything got created through Him — everything up in the heavens, everything down here on earth, stuff you can see and stuff you can't, whether it's thrones or dominions or rulers or powers — every last thing was created through Him, and for Him.

web_text: He is before all things, and in him all things are held together. He is the head of the body, the assembly, who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence. For all the fullness was pleased to dwell in him; and through him to reconcile all things to himself, by him, whether things on the earth, or things in the heavens, having made peace through the blood of his cross.
slang_text: He's already there before anything else, and everything holds together in Him. He's the head of the body — the church — He's the beginnin', the firstborn from the dead, so He'd end up first place in absolutely everything. 'Cause the whole fullness was totally stoked to live right there in Him, and through Him to patch everything back up with Himself, whether it's stuff on earth or stuff up in the heavens, makin' peace happen through the blood of His own cross.

web_text: You, being in past times alienated and enemies in your mind in your evil deeds, yet now he has reconciled in the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and without defect and blameless before him, if it is so that you continue in the faith, grounded and steadfast, and not moved away from the hope of the Good News which you heard, which is being proclaimed in all creation under heaven; of which I, Paul, was made a servant. Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake, and fill up on my part that which is lacking of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body’s sake, which is the assembly;
slang_text: You guys used to be cut off, straight-up enemies in your minds, chasin' evil deeds — but now He's patched things up through the death of His own body, so He could present you holy, spotless, and blameless right in front of Him — as long as you keep stickin' with the faith, rooted and steady, not gettin' knocked off the hope of the Good News you already heard, the same Good News gettin' proclaimed to every bit of creation under heaven, which I, Paul, got made a servant of. I'm actually stoked about my own sufferin' for your sake, fillin' in whatever's still missin' from Christ's afflictions, in my own flesh, for the sake of His body — the church;

web_text: of which I was made a servant, according to the stewardship of God which was given me toward you, to fulfill the word of God, the mystery which has been hidden for ages and generations. But now it has been revealed to his saints, to whom God was pleased to make known what are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory; whom we proclaim, admonishing every man and teaching every man in all wisdom, that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus;
slang_text: a servant I got made, accordin' to the job God handed me for your sake, to lay out God's word in full — the mystery that's been hidden away for ages and generations. But now it's been revealed to His saints, who God was stoked to let in on how loaded with glory this mystery is among the Gentiles — which is Christ livin' in you, the hope of glory; and we proclaim Him, warnin' everybody, teachin' everybody with all the wisdom we got, so we can present everybody mature and complete in Christ Jesus;

web_text: for which I also labor, striving according to his working, which works in me mightily.
slang_text: and that's what I'm bustin' my hump for, strugglin' hard, powered by His own strength workin' in me somethin' mighty.

web_text: For I desire to have you know how greatly I struggle for you, and for those at Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh; that their hearts may be comforted, they being knit together in love, and gaining all riches of the full assurance of understanding, that they may know the mystery of God, both of the Father and of Christ, in whom are all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge hidden. Now this I say that no one may delude you with persuasiveness of speech.
slang_text: I want you to know how hard I'm strugglin' for you guys, and for the folks over in Laodicea, and for everybody who's never even seen my face in person; so their hearts get comforted, knit tight together in love, grabbin' up all the riches of full-on understandin', so they can know the mystery of God, both the Father and Christ, in whom every treasure of wisdom and knowledge is hidden away. I'm sayin' all this so nobody cons you with some slick, persuasive talk.

web_text: For though I am absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, rejoicing and seeing your order, and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ. As therefore you received Christ Jesus, the Lord, walk in him, rooted and built up in him, and established in the faith, even as you were taught, abounding in it in thanksgiving. Be careful that you don’t let anyone rob you through his philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the elements of the world, and not after Christ.
slang_text: 'Cause even though I'm not there in person, I'm with you in spirit, stoked, watchin' how orderly you're stayin', how steady your faith in Christ is. So same as you took in Christ Jesus the Lord, keep walkin' in Him, rooted and built up in Him, locked into the faith the way you got taught, overflowin' with thanksgiving. Watch out, don't let nobody rip you off with some bogus philosophy and empty con job, chasin' human tradition, chasin' this world's basic rules, instead of chasin' Christ.

web_text: For in him all the fullness of the Deity dwells bodily, and in him you are made full, who is the head of all principality and power; in whom you were also circumcised with a circumcision not made with hands, in the putting off of the body of the sins of the flesh, in the circumcision of Christ; having been buried with him in baptism, in which you were also raised with him through faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead.
slang_text: 'Cause in Him, the entire fullness of God Himself lives in a real body, and in Him you're made complete too — He's the head over every ruler and power out there; in Him you got circumcised too, with a circumcision no human hands made, strippin' off your old fleshly sinful self, through Christ's own circumcision; buried with Him in baptism, and raised right back up with Him through faith in God's power, the same power that raised Him from the dead.

web_text: You were dead through your trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh. He made you alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses, wiping out the handwriting in ordinances which was against us; and he has taken it out of the way, nailing it to the cross; having stripped the principalities and the powers, he made a show of them openly, triumphing over them in it. Let no one therefore judge you in eating, or in drinking, or with respect to a feast day or a new moon or a Sabbath day,
slang_text: You were dead as a doornail, stuck in your screwups and your uncircumcised flesh. But He made you alive right along with Him, forgivin' every single one of our screwups, wipin' out that whole debt list of rules that was stacked up against us; He took it clean outta the way, nailin' it straight to the cross; He stripped the rulers and powers down bare, put 'em on public display, and paraded His victory over 'em right there on the cross. So don't let nobody judge you over what you eat or drink, or over some feast day, new moon, or Sabbath,

web_text: which are a shadow of the things to come; but the body is Christ’s. Let no one rob you of your prize by a voluntary humility and worshiping of the angels, dwelling in the things which he has not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind, and not holding firmly to the Head, from whom all the body, being supplied and knit together through the joints and ligaments, grows with God’s growth. If you died with Christ from the elements of the world, why, as though living in the world, do you subject yourselves to ordinances,
slang_text: all that stuff's just a shadow of what's comin' — the real substance is Christ. Don't let nobody swindle you outta your prize with some fake humble act and worshippin' angels, obsessin' over visions they never even had, puffed up for nothin' by their own fleshly thinkin', not holdin' tight to the Head, the one who's got the whole body supplied and knit together through joints and ligaments, growin' with God's own growth. If you already died with Christ, away from this world's basic rules, why are you still actin' like you're stuck livin' in the world, submittin' to regulations,

web_text: “Don’t handle, nor taste, nor touch” (all of which perish with use), according to the precepts and doctrines of men? Which things indeed appear like wisdom in self-imposed worship, and humility, and severity to the body; but aren’t of any value against the indulgence of the flesh.
slang_text: "Don't handle this, don't taste that, don't touch this other thing" (stuff that's gonna rot away the second you use it anyway), all based on some human rulebook and made-up teachin's? Sure, that stuff might look wise on the surface — all that self-made worship, fake humility, beatin' up your own body — but it's totally worthless against actually fightin' off fleshly cravings.

web_text: If then you were raised together with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated on the right hand of God. Set your mind on the things that are above, not on the things that are on the earth. For you died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God. When Christ, our life, is revealed, then you will also be revealed with him in glory.
slang_text: So if you got raised up with Christ, chase after the stuff that's up above, where Christ is, seated at God's right hand. Keep your mind fixed on the stuff that's up above, not the stuff down here on earth. 'Cause you already died, and your life's hidden away with Christ in God. When Christ, who's our life, gets revealed, then you're gonna get revealed right along with Him, in glory.

web_text: Put to death therefore your members which are on the earth: sexual immorality, uncleanness, depraved passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry; for which things’ sake the wrath of God comes on the children of disobedience. You also once walked in those, when you lived in them; but now you also put them all away: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and shameful speaking out of your mouth.
slang_text: So put to death whatever's earthly in you: sexual immorality, grody uncleanness, twisted passion, evil desire, and greed, which is basically idol worship; it's exactly this stuff that brings God's wrath down on the disobedient crowd. You used to walk around in that stuff too, back when you were livin' in it. But now ditch all of it: anger, rage, malice, slander, and every shameful word outta your mouth.

web_text: Don’t lie to one another, seeing that you have put off the old man with his doings, and have put on the new man, who is being renewed in knowledge after the image of his Creator, where there can’t be Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, bondservant, freeman; but Christ is all, and in all. Put on therefore, as God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, a heart of compassion, kindness, lowliness, humility, and perseverance;
slang_text: Don't lie to each other — you already stripped off your old self and its old ways, and suited up in your new self, gettin' renewed in knowledge, matchin' the image of the One who made you. There's no Greek or Jew in this deal, no circumcised or uncircumcised, no barbarian, no outsider, no slave, no free man — Christ's all that matters, and He's in everybody. So suit up, as God's own chosen ones, holy and loved, with a heart full of compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience;

web_text: bearing with one another, and forgiving each other, if any man has a complaint against any; even as Christ forgave you, so you also do. Above all these things, walk in love, which is the bond of perfection. And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to which also you were called in one body; and be thankful. Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly; in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your heart to the Lord.
slang_text: puttin' up with each other, forgivin' each other, if anybody's got a beef with somebody — same as Christ forgave you, do the same right back. And on top of all that, walk in love, which ties everything together perfectly. Let God's peace call the shots in your hearts, the same peace you got called to as one body; and stay thankful. Let Christ's word live in you loaded up rich, teachin' and warnin' each other with all the wisdom you got, through psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, singin' with gratitude in your heart to the Lord.

web_text: Whatever you do, in word or in deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father, through him. Wives, be in subjection to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord. Husbands, love your wives, and don’t be bitter against them. Children, obey your parents in all things, for this pleases the Lord.
slang_text: Whatever you do, whether it's somethin' you say or somethin' you do, do it all in the name of the Lord Jesus, givin' thanks to God the Father through Him. Wives, yield to your husbands, however that fits in the Lord. Husbands, love your wives, and don't get bitter toward 'em. Kids, obey your parents in everything, 'cause that's what pleases the Lord.

web_text: Fathers, don’t provoke your children, so that they won’t be discouraged. Servants, obey in all things those who are your masters according to the flesh, not just when they are looking, as men pleasers, but in singleness of heart, fearing God. And whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord, and not for men, knowing that from the Lord you will receive the reward of the inheritance; for you serve the Lord Christ.
slang_text: Dads, don't wind your kids up till they get discouraged. Servants, obey your earthly masters in everything, not just when they're watchin', tryin' to score points — do it with a genuine heart, fearin' God. And whatever you do, dive in hard, like you're workin' for the Lord and not for people, knowin' the Lord's gonna hand you the reward of the inheritance; 'cause you're really servin' the Lord Christ.

web_text: But he who does wrong will receive again for the wrong that he has done, and there is no partiality.
slang_text: But whoever does somebody wrong is gonna get paid back exactly what they dished out, and there's no favoritism about it, none whatsoever.

web_text: Masters, give to your servants that which is just and equal, knowing that you also have a Master in heaven. Continue steadfastly in prayer, watching therein with thanksgiving; praying together for us also, that God may open to us a door for the word, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds; that I may reveal it as I ought to speak.
slang_text: Masters, treat your servants fair and square, knowin' you've got your own Master up in heaven watchin' you too. Keep at it steady with prayer, stayin' alert and thankful about it; pray for us too, that God would crack open a door for the word, so I could speak up about Christ's mystery — the same reason I'm stuck in these chains — so I can lay it all out just like I oughta.

web_text: Walk in wisdom toward those who are outside, redeeming the time. Let your speech always be with grace, seasoned with salt, that you may know how you ought to answer each one. All my affairs will be made known to you by Tychicus, the beloved brother, faithful servant, and fellow bondservant in the Lord. I am sending him to you for this very purpose, that he may know your circumstances and comfort your hearts,
slang_text: Walk smart around the folks outside the faith, makin' the most of every chance you get. Let your talk always be full of grace, seasoned with a little salt, so you know how to answer each person right. Tychicus, our beloved brother, faithful servant, and fellow servant in the Lord, is gonna fill you in on everything goin' on with me. I'm sendin' him your way for exactly this reason, so he can find out how you're doin' and comfort your hearts,

web_text: together with Onesimus, the faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They will make known to you everything that is going on here. Aristarchus, my fellow prisoner, greets you, and Mark, the cousin of Barnabas (concerning whom you received commandments, “if he comes to you, receive him”), and Jesus who is called Justus. These are my only fellow workers for God’s Kingdom who are of the circumcision, men who have been a comfort to me. Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, salutes you, always striving for you in his prayers, that you may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.
slang_text: along with Onesimus, our faithful and beloved brother, one of your own. Those two are gonna fill you in on everything happenin' here. Aristarchus, locked up right with me, sends his greetin's, and so does Mark, Barnabas' cousin (you already got word about him — "if he shows up, welcome him in"), and Jesus, who goes by Justus. These are the only guys from the circumcised crowd still workin' with me for God's Kingdom, and man, have they been a comfort to me. Epaphras, one of your own, a servant of Christ, sends his love too, always strugglin' for you in his prayers, that you'd stand mature and complete in everything God wants.

web_text: For I testify about him, that he has great zeal for you, and for those in Laodicea, and for those in Hierapolis. Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas greet you. Greet the brothers who are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the assembly that is in his house. When this letter has been read among you, cause it to be read also in the assembly of the Laodiceans; and that you also read the letter from Laodicea.
slang_text: I'll vouch for him — he's got serious fire for you guys, and for the folks in Laodicea, and Hierapolis too. Luke, our beloved doctor, and Demas send their greetin's. Greet the brothers over in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the church that meets at his house. Once this letter's been read among you, make sure it gets read to the Laodicean church too, and that you read the letter from Laodicea as well.

web_text: Tell Archippus, “Take heed to the ministry which you have received in the Lord, that you fulfill it.” The salutation of me, Paul, with my own hand: remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen.
slang_text: Tell Archippus: "Pay attention to the ministry you got handed in the Lord, and make sure you finish it out." This is me, Paul, signin' off with my own hand: remember I'm still in chains. Grace be with you. Amen.
